channel.title: Anish Reflection blog
channel.description: Recent content on Anish Reflection blog

item.title: Reflection on 1st unit
item.description: When I was introduced to this course I was the happiest person since I wanted to learn and explore more. In the beginning I learnt how to synthesize the information and filter it for particular work. I got to know that this course is a little challenging but the instructor helped in the understanding to achieve the course requirement. Further I was introduced to Zettler on which we were asked to work on. Afterwards I got to know about GitHud.  Essentially I was totally new and unknown to the topic GitHub and didn’t know much about the developers who share their work online. Further I got to know that GitHub is mainly used for storing code and collaborating on software projects, so writing a blog using GitHub was completely new and challenging for a student like me. In the beginning the interface was a little confusing and some of the words which made me uncomfortable were the repositories, commits, and branches. I was unsure how these concepts were connected to building a blog.To be honest, during the setting of the blog on GitHub some of the codes which were making me uncomfortable, through which I learnt about the platform and their function. The very first step which I did was to create a repository and added files to it. I also learned that writing blog posts can be done using Markdown, which is a simple way to format text. Initially, it was challenging to understand how to upload files and make the blog visible online. However, after following the instructions step by step, I realized that GitHub can automatically publish a website using GitHub Pages. Seeing the blog appear online for the first time was very satisfying.This opportunity gave me a sense of understanding how to fix the problem of coding and till now I get a little idea how to work on github. Even the process was a little challenging in the beginning but asking my peers and instructor who made my doubts clear and helped  me out.

item.title: Reflection on 2nd unit
item.description: My Learning Experience with Image Formation Before this course, I was having a basic idea about the image, and never thought that this topic would have such a deep concept of how exactly an image forms and how it undergoes transformation, filtration and other changes. The most interesting part was when I played with black and white image formation and changing its pixel. The concept of pixels, which are the tiny units of an image and  memory, which holds the image’s capacity were also totally new for me to process.Earlier I knew that images have two types: the color and the black and white image. I learnt how images form and how we can change its intensity to get different compositions of it. The color image is the composition of mainly three colors that are red, green and blue from which all images form. When I used to see the color image in HD I used to wonder how it is more clearer than other normal images. After going through this topic I understood how it works. The concept of filtration was also one of ideas which I used to be curious about.  I have been using the many editing applications on my device , but never knew what exactly was happening inside. For the user it was so easy to   bring different types of transformation to Image, on the other hand a lot of logical mathematics  goes into it. Upon changing the digital image process , my perception of the editing world has changed completely.Still there are some questions in my mind that, how do I get a movie file with a 2 or 3 gb when the actual storage of a simple 4k movie consumes more than 10 GB.

item.title: My First Post
item.description: Hi, I’m Anish Kumar. I’m someone who’s curious to know the unknown. Currently I am in Azim Premji University for Bachalors(Hons) Degree in Informaiton Science. I enjoy learning, questioning ideas, and make connections. For me, education isn’t just about textbooks or exams; it’s about understanding society, power, and change in a deeper way.I am deeply interested in bunch of activities like painting, playing sports like badmintion, singing, a bit of dancing and traveling. Beyond academics, I value clarity, consistency, and curiosity. I try to approach new ideas with an open mind and a willingness to learn, even when they challenge my existing views. This blog is a small space where I share thoughts, reflections, and ideas as I continue that journey.
